summary: Rati dropped a massive 6,400-line update on *Orchard*, transforming the app with a sleek new multi-page control panel, secure passkey auth, and deep iCloud device integration.
Rati took a break from heavy coding to bless the `rvardiashvili/GSIP` repository with 57 lines of doc clarifications—proving that a little clarity goes a very long way.
Rati performed a surgical strike on the GSIP Prithvi adapter today, tightening up error handling with a lean update that actually left the codebase two lines lighter than he found it.

Rati turned GSIP into a fortress with a 3,140-line testing marathon, deploying 228 pytests across 15 modules. He even fixed Python 3.9 compatibility—because nothing says "quality" like a massive, bug-squashing coverage boost.

Rati catapulted GSIP to v0.2.0 today, pushing nearly 2,000 lines to sync documentation with reality, refine CLI hints, and harden CI. He’s clearly not just coding; he’s meticulously engineering a production-ready machine.

Rati practically rewrote GSIP today, dropping 11,000 lines to ship a modular plugin system, YOLO-powered object detection, and hardened CI, effectively turning a niche tool into a production-ready geospatial beast.

Rati performed surgical micro-fixes on `bloom-installer` today. In just six lines, he banished "rainbow" bootloader corruption and finicky syntax errors, proving that even a tiny diff can save the startup UI.
